title: Rotorua on a Budget
meta_desc: Discover how to visit Rotorua with your family without breaking the bank. Get the inside scoop on affordable activities, accommodation, and daily costs for a family of four.
intro: Rotorua is a fantastic destination for families, with geothermal wonders, Maori culture, and plenty of activities to keep everyone entertained. With some planning, you can have a great time in Rotorua without overspending. Here's a budget guide to help you make the most of your trip.

sections.heading: Daily Costs
sections.body:
A family of four can expect to pay around $250-450 per day in Rotorua, depending on their accommodation choices and activities. This budget can cover accommodation, food, and activities, but it's always a good idea to have some extra money set aside for souvenirs or unexpected expenses.

Some of the top activities in Rotorua include Te Puia, Wai-O-Tapu Thermal Wonderland, and the Skyline Rotorua Gondola & Luge. These activities can cost between $20-50 per person, but they're definitely worth the cost.
list: Skyline Rotorua Gondola & Luge: $30-$50 per person

sections.heading: Accommodation Options
sections.body:
There are plenty of accommodation options in Rotorua to suit all budgets. You can choose from budget-friendly motels, mid-range hotels, or luxury resorts. Some popular options include the Rotorua Top 10 Holiday Resort, the Novotel Rotorua Lakeside, and the Polynesian Spa Resort.

Prices for accommodation in Rotorua can range from $100-500 per night, depending on the type of accommodation and the time of year.

sections.heading: Free Activities
sections.body:
One of the best things about Rotorua is the number of free activities available. You can visit the Government Gardens and croquet lawn, take a walk along the Lake Rotorua foreshore, or explore the Kuirau Park, which is a free public geothermal park with hot bubbling mud pools and steam vents.

These free activities are a great way to experience the natural beauty of Rotorua without breaking the bank.
